headline: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm are all teasing Nvidia’s new N1X laptop processors
summary: Nvidia is widely expected to announce its new Arm-powered laptop chips at Computex. Microsoft, Nvidia, and Arm are openly teasing a "new era of PC." The N1X processors are anticipated to be a major development for laptops.

headline: SpaceX gets $4 billion contract to build missile-tracking ‘Golden Dome’ satellites
summary: SpaceX secured a $4.16 billion contract from the Pentagon. The deal is to build missile-tracking satellites for the "Golden Dome" defense system. These satellites will detect and track targets from space for the US Space Force.

headline: Acer’s launching a Linux handheld for streaming your PC games
summary: Acer announced the Nitro Blaze Link, a Linux-based handheld streaming device. It's designed specifically for streaming PC games, akin to a PlayStation Portal. The device features a 7-inch display and is set for a Q4 2026 launch.

headline: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
summary: An AI training startup, Shift, offers free home cleaning in exchange for video footage. The collected footage is used to train AI and robot systems for household tasks. Shift plans to expand its controversial service to other major cities.

headline: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-Winning Star Wars and Return of the Jedi Editor, Dies at 80
summary: Oscar-winning film editor Marcia Lucas has passed away at the age of 80. She was renowned for her work on "Star Wars" and "Return of the Jedi." Lucas was also the ex-wife of "Star Wars" creator George Lucas.

headline: No Spacesuits? No Problem! The For All Mankind Spin-Off Star City is a Very Different Kind of Space Exploration Show
summary: "Star City" is a new spin-off from the popular "For All Mankind" series. It focuses on the Russian perspective of the alternate-history space race. The show promises a unique take on space exploration, differing from its parent series.

headline: Toy Story 5 Has "One of the Most Heartbreaking Scenes" in the Franchise, Tom Hanks Says
summary: Tom Hanks, voice of Woody, teases a "heartbreaking scene" for "Toy Story 5." He suggests it will be one of the most emotional moments in the 30-year franchise. Fans anticipate a poignant addition to the beloved Pixar series.

headline: Spider-Man: Brand New Day's Punisher Is "Authentic to the Frank Castle We Know," Tom Holland Says
summary: Tom Holland reassures fans about the Punisher's portrayal in "Spider-Man: Brand New Day." He states the character will be "authentic to the Frank Castle we know." The comment addresses concerns about the Punisher's depiction in the upcoming series.

headline: Palace was handed Andrew's controversial envoy emails six years ago
summary: Thousands of emails concerning Prince Andrew's financial dealings were given to the Royal Household. These controversial communications were reportedly handed over in 2020. The revelation raises questions about the Palace's prior knowledge of the prince's affairs.

headline: No deal announced after Trump meeting to make 'final determination' on Iran
summary: No agreement was announced following President Trump's meeting with advisors on Iran. Officials had previously indicated a framework deal between the US and Iran. The meeting was intended to make a "final determination" on the matter.

headline: Arrive three hours before flight home, airline boss tells UK holidaymakers
summary: Wizz Air CEO advises UK holidaymakers to arrive three hours early for flights. Passengers are also urged to carry portable chargers and water. The advice comes amid expectations of lengthy queues and potential travel delays.

headline: Millions of breast cancer patients could safely avoid chemotherapy, study suggests
summary: A new international trial suggests many breast cancer patients may not need chemotherapy. A DNA test could help determine who can safely forego the aggressive treatment. This discovery could potentially spare millions from chemotherapy side effects.
